title: Running Totals Using Formulas
excerpt: Has anyone figured out a way to get a running total? I saw some clever workarounds from a few years ago. But hoping someone figured out an easier way. Please help. I feel like this should be a feature in Monday.com alrea…

posts.author: erikart18 (Erika Tijerina)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone figured out a way to get a running total? I saw some clever workarounds from a few years ago. But hoping someone figured out an easier way. Please help. I feel like this should be a feature in Monday.com already.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @erikart18,

What do you mean exactly?

posts.author: mcoleman84 (Megan Coleman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love this - I’m trying to summarize the avg price of each contracted cookie - ie, I have 3 contracts, for 3 different values and 3 different quantities of cookies.

I want to take my total contract value divided by my total quantity of cookies purchased.

posts.author: erikart18 (Erika Tijerina)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to track a total budget, for example, $5,000, and have each task or project associated with a specific contract subtract its own cost from that total. I understand I can set up a running total, but what I’m aiming for is to see how much budget remains for the contract after each task deduction. Any suggestions on how to best set this up?

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
A contract is an item? A group of items? A board?
A task is therefore a sub-item? An item inside the group?

posts.author: erikart18 (Erika Tijerina)
posts.cookedHtml: Right now I am doing a group for each budget line. So, by group would be fine.
